meta.description: Daily LLM Webapp Ideas — one researched idea per day, valuable for humans and AI agents

ideas.title: LivingDocs
ideas.tagline: Auto-generated, always-fresh agent-readable knowledge layers for any website or product
ideas.one_liner: robots.txt was for Google. LivingDocs is for AI agents. One line of code and your product is readable by every AI in the world.
ideas.problem: AI agents are blind to most of the web. Only 5–15% of websites have implemented llms.txt, and most implementations are static, manually maintained files that go stale within weeks. Google, AWS, and Microsoft have all shipped MCP servers for their own docs — mid-size SaaS and open-source projects have the same need but no budget to build this themselves.
ideas.idea: A webapp that takes any URL, sitemap, or GitHub repo and automatically generates a continuously-synced agent-readable knowledge layer: a structured llms.txt, llms-full.txt, and an optional hosted MCP server endpoint — all kept fresh via webhooks or scheduled re-indexing.

value.for_humans: Product shows up accurately in Cursor, Claude, and Copilot. Higher AI-referred conversion (ChatGPT converts at 15.9%).
value.for_agents: Clean, token-efficient, semantic MCP entry point. No HTML parsing, no stale snapshots.
value.for_builders: Gartner says 30%+ of new API demand in 2026 comes from LLM tools — this is infrastructure for that wave.
value.moat: Value compounds — the longer a site is indexed, the richer the version history.

weeks: Week 1: SvelteKit + URL crawler → chunk + embed with OpenAI text-embedding-3-small → Postgres/pgvector
Week 2: LLM pass (GPT-4o-mini) to generate versioned llms.txt + llms-full.txt
Week 3: MCP-compatible endpoint (search_document + get_document tools), deploy via Railway
Week 4: Webhook sync (GitHub, Notion, Gitbook) + agent traffic analytics dashboard

pitch.icp: Developer tools, SaaS products with public docs, open-source maintainers, internal tooling teams at 50–500 person companies
pitch.gtm: Product Hunt + Hacker News Show HN. Open-source the llms.txt generator. Monetize MCP hosting, sync automation, and analytics.

pricing.team: $79/mo — unlimited sites, MCP endpoint, webhook triggers, version history

pitch.investor_angle: API infra for the agent economy. Comparable to how Algolia became essential for search — LivingDocs becomes essential for agent discoverability.

signals: Fern, Mintlify, and Scalar compete on llms.txt generation — but only for API docs platforms, not the general web
Google launched its Developer Knowledge API (MCP) in Feb 2026. AWS and Microsoft followed.
Only 5–15% of websites have llms.txt today

ideas.title: ToolGate
ideas.tagline: Approval gates, schema contracts, and audit logs for AI agents using tools
ideas.one_liner: IAM for AI agents. Every tool call gets a policy, an approval gate, and a log.
ideas.problem: AI agents can now call tools — send emails, create PRs, query databases, make purchases. But there is no standard way to enforce who approved what, what schemas are allowed, or to produce an audit trail. Observability tools trace after the fact, but nobody provides an app-agnostic policy gateway that enforces allow/deny/approval-required at runtime with typed schema contracts.
ideas.idea: A webapp that acts as a policy gateway for AI agent tool calls. It sits between agents and their tools, enforcing typed JSON Schema contracts, routing high-risk actions through human approval gates (Slack, email, dashboard), and producing audit-grade logs queryable by both humans and other agents.

value.for_enterprises: Ship agents to production with confidence. Every tool call has a policy, an approval flow, and a log. SOC 2 and ISO 27001 auditors get the paper trail they need.
value.for_agents: Machine-readable policy responses. Agents know exactly which tools they can use, what parameters are allowed, and whether to wait for approval.
value.for_developers: Drop-in middleware. Works with any MCP server, OpenAI function calling, or custom tool framework. One config file, zero code changes.

weeks: Week 1: Lightweight HTTP proxy + policy engine (YAML rules, JSON Schema validation) → Postgres
Week 2: Approval flow — hold in Redis, Slack/email notification, /approve/:id endpoint, timeout + escalation
Week 3: Audit log + SvelteKit dashboard — real-time feed, filterable, JSON/CSV export for compliance
Week 4: TypeScript SDK, Express/Hono middleware, /capabilities endpoint for agent policy discovery, Railway deploy

pitch.icp: Engineering teams shipping agents to production, platform teams managing multi-agent systems, compliance officers at regulated enterprises, MCP server authors
pitch.gtm: Open-source the proxy + policy engine. Show HN launch. Free for single-agent use. Monetize team dashboards, SSO, advanced approval workflows, compliance exports.
pitch.investor_angle: IAM for the agent economy. Every enterprise deploying agents will need a policy layer — ToolGate is the natural chokepoint. Comparable to how Okta became essential for human identity.

signals: Browser agents are less safe than chat models and need runtime guardrails (Invariant Labs)
MCP and structured tool schemas are making tool contracts a first-class product surface
Enterprises want approval and audit trails for write actions before shipping agents to production

sources.name: Invariant Labs — Enhancing Browser Agent Safety
Agent Patterns — Human Approval For AI Agents
Moveworks — Policy Validators for AI Compliance

ideas.title: SpecPulse
ideas.tagline: Continuous agent-contract tests for APIs — publish what agents can safely do, and prove it stays true.
ideas.one_liner: CI for agent tools: publish an agent contract, then continuously prove it still works.
ideas.problem: Teams are shipping agents that call real APIs (payments, support, CRM, infra). But the ‘interface’ agents rely on is a mix of human docs, stale examples, and tribal knowledge. When an endpoint changes, a scope requirement shifts, or rate limits tighten, agents fail in production — or worse, do the wrong thing. There’s no standard place to publish a machine-checkable contract for agents, and no continuous test that says: ‘an agent can still complete task X safely with tool Y’.
ideas.idea: SpecPulse is a webapp that turns your API + workflows into **agent contracts**: machine-readable capability specs (JSON Schema for parameters/outputs, auth scopes, rate limits, safety constraints, example calls) paired with **continuous contract tests** that run on a schedule and on every deploy. It publishes an agent-facing ‘capabilities feed’ and optional MCP/OpenAPI adapters, so humans can review changes and AI agents can automatically discover what’s safe and supported today.

value.for_humans: Fewer broken automations and fewer incident tickets. Product and platform teams get a clear, versioned ‘what agents can do’ surface, plus change alerts when a deploy breaks a contract test. Compliance teams get evidence that risky workflows are gated and validated.
value.for_agents: A stable, queryable capabilities feed with typed schemas and freshness signals. Agents can select tools by guaranteed-supported tasks, request just-in-time scopes, and adapt when a contract version changes.
value.compounding_moat: As more workflows are tested, SpecPulse becomes the source of truth for ‘agent-safe’ operations and their historical reliability. The contract test corpus becomes a defensible dataset that improves recommendations and auto-generated adapters.

weeks: Week 1: Data model + UI skeleton (SvelteKit) — APIs, Workflows, Contracts. Ingest OpenAPI + auth metadata. Store in Postgres.
Week 2: Contract runner — define workflow tests (YAML/JSON), run via worker (Node + Playwright/HTTP). Record results, latency, error traces.
Week 3: Publish capabilities — /capabilities.json with JSON Schema, versioning, and freshness. Generate MCP tool definitions + SDK snippet.
Week 4: Alerts + diffs — GitHub webhook, deployment hooks, Slack/email alerts, ‘what changed’ contract diff view. Ship hosted demo + docs.

pitch.icp: API-first SaaS (B2B), platform teams shipping internal agents, and developer experience teams that maintain OpenAPI specs and SDKs.
pitch.gtm: Start with an OpenAPI ‘agent contract’ linter + GitHub Action (free). Use it to upsell the hosted dashboard, continuous runners, capabilities feed hosting, and MCP adapter generation. Launch via ‘Show HN’, DX communities, and partner with API doc tools.

pricing.free: 1 API, 10 workflow tests, daily runs, public capabilities feed
pricing.pro: $39/mo — 5 APIs, hourly runs, Slack alerts, private feed
pricing.team: $149/mo — unlimited APIs, SSO, audit exports, role-based approvals

pitch.investor_angle: As agents become a primary customer of APIs, ‘agent readiness’ becomes a new reliability layer. SpecPulse is the monitoring + contract system for that layer — a chokepoint between API changes and autonomous execution.

signals: Agent governance discussions are shifting from ‘chat safety’ to ‘autonomous execution safety’.
OpenAPI is necessary but insufficient: agents need task-level workflow guarantees, scopes, and freshness signals.
Enterprises increasingly require auditability and operational visibility for agent actions — contracts make that measurable.

sources.name: Cerbos — Permission Management for AI Agents

ideas.title: ComplyCraft
ideas.tagline: An agent-ready compliance evidence workspace that collects, attests, and serves audit evidence for SMBs
ideas.one_liner: Compliance evidence on autopilot. Collect once, serve to auditors and AI agents forever.
ideas.problem: Every growing startup hits the compliance wall: security questionnaires, SOC 2 audits, finance reviews. Teams scramble through Notion, Drive, and Slack to assemble evidence manually. Enterprise GRC tools cost $15K+/yr and are overkill for SMBs. AI agents handling security reviews have no structured evidence to reference.
ideas.idea: A lightweight compliance evidence workspace that auto-collects evidence from existing tools (GitHub, AWS, Google Workspace), attests it with cryptographic hashes for chain-of-custody, and serves it through both a human dashboard and a machine-readable MCP API.

value.for_smbs: Answer security questionnaires and audit requests in hours instead of weeks. No $15K/yr GRC platform needed.
value.for_agents: Machine-readable evidence inventory. Agents can query freshness, attestation status, and cite evidence directly.
value.trust_layer: Cryptographic hashes on every artifact create chain-of-custody. Auditors trust it. Agents can verify it programmatically.

weeks: Week 1: Evidence model (Postgres) + 10 pre-built recipe templates + SvelteKit dashboard
Week 2: OAuth connectors (GitHub, AWS, Google Workspace) + S3 storage + SHA-256 hashing
Week 3: REST + MCP endpoint (list_evidence, get_evidence, check_control_status)
Week 4: Evidence package export (PDF + shared links) + scheduled re-collection + staleness alerts

pitch.icp: Seed to Series B startups (10-200 people), engineering leads and ops managers who own compliance without a dedicated team
pitch.gtm: Open-source the evidence recipe format + CLI collector. Show HN launch. Template library as content marketing. Monetize hosted dashboard, MCP endpoint, scheduled collection, shareable packages.
pitch.investor_angle: Compliance infrastructure for the SMB tier. Vanta and Secureframe own enterprise — nobody owns the lightweight, agent-native layer for smaller teams.

signals: AWS published a reference architecture for AI-powered compliance evidence collection
Enterprise GRC tools ($15K+/yr) are overkill for SMBs — massive underserved market
AI agents are starting to handle security reviews but lack structured evidence sources
